data.title: Ann Arbor entrepreneurs Matt and Shelley Crawford launching Pilates Addiction studio with WundaFormer classes, opening early spring.
data.description: Coming soon to Ann Arbor, Michigan, is a new Pilates studio brought to the community by spouses Matt and Shelley Crawford. The entrepreneurial duo will bring high-energy, results-oriented Pilates sessions, featuring WundaFormer classes. The studio is set to open its doors on January 23, 2026. This dynamic Pilates experience will offer the community a fresh approach to fitness, catering to those seeking transformative workouts. Stay tuned as the duo prepares to launch their unique Pilates venture in the bustling city.

data.title: Investors who lost money from CRWV should contact Robbins LLP about their rights. Class action filed for those who acquired CoreWeave securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, in San Diego, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit filed for investors who bought or acquired CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRWV) securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025. CoreWeave claims to be an...

data.title: Investigation into ON24, Inc. sale to Cvent by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC due to adequacy of price and process. (Numbers N/A)
data.description: A probe is being conducted regarding the planned acquisition of ON24, Inc. (NYSE: ONTF) by Cvent, as announced in New York and New Orleans on January 23, 2026. The investigation is led by former Louisiana Attorney General Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq., along with the legal firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC. The terms of the proposed sale are being scrutinized, with a focus on ensuring fairness and transparency throughout the process.

data.title: Urgent call for international protection for Bobi Wine after threats from President Museveni's son Muhoozi Kainerugaba.
data.description: Amsterdam & Partners LLP, a longstanding international legal counsel for Ugandan opposition figure Bobi Wine, has issued a concerning alert about the public warnings directed at Mr. Wine by General Muhoozi Kainerugaba. The latter, who is the son of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni and holds a senior position in the country's security organization, recently made threats against Mr. Wine following the January 15th election in Uganda. These statements are deemed as a serious and plausible risk to Mr. Wine's safety and well-being.

data.title: Japan's tremors prompt US Treasury to consider currency intervention. New York Fed inquiries hint at potential large yen purchase.
data.description: The New York Federal Reserve's recent inquiries regarding the expenses involved in swapping yen for dollars have indicated to traders that there is a possibility of the U.S. government engaging in a significant acquisition of the Japanese currency. This development has caught the attention of market participants and sparked speculation about potential upcoming transactions involving considerable amounts of yen and dollars.

data.title: Colombian National Tax and Customs Authority notifies Ecopetrol of Resolution 000571 related to VAT proceedings on fuel imports.
data.description: Colombia's leading petroleum company, Ecopetrol S.A., disclosed receiving Resolution 000571 from the Colombian National Tax and Customs Authority ("DIAN") on January 22, 2026. The resolution's details were announced by the Company the following day. Ecopetrol, listed on the Colombian stock exchange as ECOPETROL and on the New York Stock Exchange as EC, confirmed the tax-related development.

data.title: Alert for BBWI stockholders: Class action against Bath & Body Works, Inc. filed for losses between June 4, 2024 and November 19, 2025. Contact Robbins LLP for rights info.
data.description: In San Diego, a legal firm, Robbins LLP, issues a reminder about a class action lawsuit for investors who bought or acquired Bath & Body Works, Inc. (NYSE: BBWI) stocks between June 4, 2024, and November 19, 2025. The lawsuit covers all individuals involved during this period and concerns potential securities law violations. Bath & Body Works is a prominent company in the industry.

data.title: Investor alert for Two Harbors Investment Corp. as Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC investigate sale to UWM Holdings Corporation.
data.description: Investigations are being carried out by Former Attorney General of Louisiana Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq. and law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC regarding the potential sale of Two Harbors Investment Corp. (NYSE: TWO) to UWM Holdings Corporation (NYSE:...). The inquiry is focused on determining whether the sale is advantageous for shareholders. Specific details about the transaction and the companies involved have not been disclosed in the provided information.

data.title: Investors who suffered losses in ARDT stock should contact Robbins LLP regarding a class action for those who purchased ARDT securities between July 18, 2025, and November 12, 2025.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to shareholders regarding a class action lawsuit filed for individuals who bought or acquired Ardent Health, Inc. (NYSE: ARDT) stocks between July 18, 2025, and November 12, 2025. The lawsuit involves allegations against Ardent Health and its associated companies.

data.title: Investigation launched by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC on sale of DigitalBridge Group, Inc. (NYSE: DBRG) to SoftBank Group Corp.
data.description: Investigation into the potential acquisition of DigitalBridge Group, Inc. (NYSE: DBRG) by SoftBank Group Corp is underway by former Louisiana Attorney General Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq. and the law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC. The acquisition proposal is being scrutinized for its terms and implications. The news was reported on Jan. 23, 2026, in New York and New Orleans. Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq. and the firm are focusing on assessing the deal's details, considering the significant impact it could have on various stakeholders.

data.title: Investor alert: KSF investigates Clearwater Analytics Holdings sale to Permira and Warburg Pincus. Charles C. Foti leading investigation.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, Charles C. Foti, Jr., former Attorney General of Louisiana, and the law firm Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC announced an investigation into the acquisition of Clearwater Analytics Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CWAN) by Permira and Warburg Pincus. The investigation focuses on analyzing the proposed sale and ensuring the fairness of the transaction for shareholders. Clearwater Analytics Holdings, Inc. is a company listed on the New York Stock Exchange with an undisclosed market capitalization. Charles C. Foti, Jr. and Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC are renowned for their expertise in handling such investigations.

data.title: Andreessen Horowitz secures $15B for American Dynamism; Powell-Trump disputes; Crypto sees minimal movement: btc at $90,600, eth +1% at $3,110, sol +2% at $140, xrp -2% to $2.04; XMR hits ATH at $590. Fed Chair Powell refutes collusion charges; X unveils "smart cashtags" for live prices; Vaneck projects BTC at $53M by 2050. Ripple approved for UK payments; BNY Mellon introduces tokenized deposits. House bill restricts officials from prediction markets; Tether freezes $182M.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies are experiencing minimal movement as the market opens on Monday, with Bitcoin standing steady at $90,600. Ethereum is up by 1% at $3,110, while Solana has seen a 2% increase at $140; however, XRP has dropped by 2% to $2.04. Notably, IP surged by 20% and XMR by 15%, with XMR setting a new all-time high at $590. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell addressed his criminal charges in a video, attributing them to his refusal to align interest rates with Trump’s demands. X, on Twitter, revealed plans for introducing "smart cashtags" to display live crypto and stock prices next to tickers. Vaneck forecasts Bitcoin to potentially reach $53 million by 2050, driven by long-term adoption and growth assumptions. Andreessen Horowitz raised $15 billion to support American Dynamism, focusing on AI and crypto. Ripple obtained FCA approval to expand crypto payments in the UK. BNY Mellon launched tokenized deposits for institutional and digital-native clients. A new house bill aims to restrict lawmakers and federal officials from participating in prediction markets. Tether froze $182 million in USDT tied to Venezuelan oil trades.

data.title: ZEC surges 11%; Trump won't pardon Sam Bankman-Fried; FL reconsiders BTC Reserve. Major cryptos flat pre-trump tariffs court decision; BTC at $90,300 (+1%); ETH at $3,090 (+13%); SOL at $138 (+3%); XRP at $2.10 (+1%). Top movers: Polygon (+11%), ZEC (+11%), and Syrup (+7%). JPMorgan thinks BTC & ETH sell-off might be ending due to better positioning. Bank of America upgrades Coinbase to buy; Morgan Stanley plans digital wallet launch supporting tokenized assets. Florida renews push for state-level BTC reserve; Ethereum’s validator queue cleared. Polygon Labs introduces open money stack initiative; close to acquiring Coinme. Trump won't pardon FTX founder.
data.description: Cryptocurrency majors are experiencing minimal movement ahead of an upcoming Supreme Court opinion on Trump tariffs. Bitcoin is up 1% at $90,300, while Ethereum is seeing a 13% increase at $3,090. Solana is up 3% at $138, and XRP has risen by 1% to $2.10. Top movers include Polygon (+11%), Zcash (+11%), and Syrup (+7%). JPMorgan suggests that the recent sell-off in Bitcoin and Ethereum may be reaching its bottom, with improving positioning and easing downside pressure. Bank of America analysts have upgraded Coinbase to buy, citing improved regulatory clarity and growing institutional adoption. Morgan Stanley plans to launch a digital wallet supporting tokenized assets later this year. Florida lawmakers are pushing for a state-level Bitcoin reserve. Ethereum's validator exit queue has cleared, easing delays in liquid staking protocols. Polygon Labs introduced the Open Money Stack to streamline stablecoin payments. Polygon is nearing the acquisition of Coinme, a major Bitcoin ATM operator. Trump has stated he will not pardon FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried.

data.title: BTC dips below $90K, ZEC plunges 19% post developer team resignation over a dispute with the board. Crypto majors take a hit: BTC -2% to $89,900, ETH -3% to $3,100, SOL -3% to $134, XRP -7% to $2.08. JPM Coin to launch on Canton network, Barclays invests in UBYX. Senate banking committee under pressure for upcoming legislation vote. Wyoming introduces Frontier Stable Token, StarkNet faces outage due to block bug.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies experienced a decline in value yesterday, with Bitcoin (BTC) dropping by $90,000 to reach $89,900, a 2% decrease. Ethereum (ETH) also saw a 3% decrease, standing at $3,100, while Solana (SOL) dropped by 3% to $134, and XRP decreased by 7% to $2.08. Among the top gainers were Lit (+3%), WLFI (+3%), and XMR (+3%). However, Zcash (ZEC) plummeted by 19% following the resignation of the developer team amidst a disagreement with the board. The team intends to establish a new company to continue their work. JPMorgan revealed its plans to introduce JPM Coin on the Canton network. Barclays made an investment in UBYX, a US stablecoin settlement startup, supporting the development of infrastructure enabling regulated institutions to transfer digital funds between issuers and wallets. The Senate Banking Committee is under pressure ahead of a crucial vote on crypto market structure legislation next week. Wyoming launched its inaugural state-backed stablecoin, Frontier Stable Token, which is now accessible to the public. A subsidiary of World Liberty Financial applied for a national trust bank charter to issue and protect its USD-backed stablecoin within a federally regulated framework. Starknet encountered a multi-hour outage due to a block production bug, causing the network to pause and rollback before returning to normal operation.

data.title: Crypto news: First 2026 dip as BTC hits $92K! Morgan Stanley files for BTC, ETH & SOL ETFs. Hyperliquid airdrop buzz!
data.description: In a significant turn of events, major cryptocurrencies experienced a decline for the first time in 2026. Bitcoin (BTC) saw a 2% drop, resting at $92,000, while Ethereum (ETH) and Solana (SOL) decreased by 1% each, settling at $3,210 and $138, respectively. Ripple (XRP) also dipped by 5% to $2.24. Morgan Stanley made waves by filing for ETFs for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana. The U.S. Senate Banking Committee is gearing up for a crucial vote on a bill concerning crypto market structure in the following week. Additionally, the Ethereum network observed a surge in activity, with over 2 million daily transactions recorded. Noteworthy announcements include Nike's sale of RTFKT and Clone X's astounding 250% leap. Telegram revealed the sale of $450 million worth of its TON over the past year. Hyperliquid unveiled a progress map, fueling speculation about an imminent airdrop.

data.title: Crypto market cap hits $3.22T; BTC at $93,780; ETH at $3,240; SOL at $139. Major moves: XRP +12%, RENDER +18%, SUI +18%, LIT +15%. Key news: Bank of America suggests 4% crypto allocation, Morgan Stanley files Solana Trust, Japan endorses deeper integration. Vitalik Buterin touts Ethereum's resolution of Blockchain trilemma. Security concerns: Kraken probes data sale on dark web, Ledger faces breach with Global-E.
data.description: The total value of the global cryptocurrency market increased by 2% to reach $3.22 trillion, with leading cryptocurrencies experiencing gains: Bitcoin rose by 1% to $93,780, Ethereum increased by 2% to $3,240, and Solana surged by 3% to $139. XRP saw the highest growth, jumping by 12% to $2.37, while RENDER, SUI, and LIT were the top-performing assets, with gains of 18%, 18%, and 15% respectively. Bank of America unveiled its crypto investment suggestions for affluent customers, advising a potential 4% allotment in portfolios. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley submitted a filing to establish a Solana Trust under the supervision of the SEC. Goldman Sachs raised Coinbase's rating to 'Buy' but downgraded eToro. Japan's finance minister expressed support for deeper integration of cryptocurrencies, proposing reduced taxes and reforms at exchange level. Vitalik Buterin highlighted that Ethereum has effectively addressed the "Blockchain Trilemma" by maintaining a balance between decentralization and scalability through its Layer-2 strategy. Kraken is probing alleged reports of customer data being traded on the dark web after a suspected security breach, while Ledger users are vigilant as the hardware wallet provider faces a data breach involving its e-commerce partner, Global-E, potentially exposing customer contact information.

data.title: Crypto market cap at $3.22T, XRP surges 12% to $2.37. Japan approves crypto. Fear & Greed at Neutral. Bank of America recommends 4% crypto portfolio. Morgan Stanley files for Solana Trust. Goldman Sachs upgrades Coinbase. Japan endorses crypto integration. Ledger faces data breach.
data.description: The global cryptocurrency market cap saw a 2% increase to reach $3.22 trillion, with Bitcoin (BTC) up by 1% at $93,780 and Ethereum (ETH) climbing 2% to $3,240. Solana (SOL) also experienced a 3% increase, reaching $139, while XRP surged by 12% to reach $2.37. Among the top movers were RENDER (+18%), SUI (+18%), and LIT (+15%). Bank of America introduced crypto recommendations for wealth clients, suggesting up to a 4% allocation in portfolios. Morgan Stanley submitted a filing with the SEC for a Solana Trust. Goldman Sachs raised Coinbase's rating to ‘Buy’ but downgraded eToro. Japan’s finance minister expressed support for increased crypto integration, proposing lower taxes and exchange reforms. Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum has effectively addressed the “Blockchain Trilemma” by balancing decentralization and scalability through the Layer-2 roadmap. Kraken is looking into reports of customer data being sold on the dark web due to a possible security breach. Ledger users are on high alert after a data breach involving their e-commerce partner Global-E led to the exposure of customer contact details.

data.title: Crypto market cap hits $3.16t (+1.5%), btc at $93,000 (+2%), eth at $3,175 (+1%). Top gainers: Pepe +67%, Virtuals +24%, btc etfs see $471m inflows. PwC deepens crypto focus.
data.description: The cryptocurrency market has reached a global market cap of $3.16 trillion, demonstrating a 1.5% increase. Major cryptocurrencies are seeing gains, with Bitcoin (BTC) rising by 2% to $93,000, Ethereum (ETH) increasing by 1% to $3,175, Binance Coin (BNB) surging by 2.5% to $906, and Solana (SOL) edging up by 1% to $135. Among the top performers are Virtuals with a 24% increase, Render up by 17%, and BitTorrent (BTT) and Fetch.ai (FET) both growing by 11%. Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) experienced $471 million in net inflows on the first day of trading in 2026, marking the highest single-day total since November 11. SEC Commissioner Caroline Crenshaw officially left the agency on January 2, resulting in an all-Republican commission. PwC, one of the Big 4 accounting firms, revealed plans to expand further into the cryptocurrency space, with a particular emphasis on stablecoins and payment solutions.

data.title: Crypto wallets could rival neobanks.Bitget Wallet evolves into an everyday finance app merging crypto with TradFi, aiming to make "Crypto for Everyone."
data.description: Jamie Elkaleh, CMO of Bitget Wallet, discussed with Decrypt the platform's progression from a cryptocurrency wallet to an all-encompassing financial application designed to effortlessly merge cryptocurrency with traditional finance. This development aligns with their goal of making cryptocurrency accessible to all individuals, following the motto "Crypto for Everyone."

data.title: Tom Lee buys $130M ETH, holds $1B cash. Btc: 87k | Eth: 2975 | bnb: 855. Rekt, WorldStar collab teased. BlackRock BUIDL hits $100M dividends, over $2B assets. Metaplanet buys 4,279 BTC, now 35,102. Unleash Protocol hit by $3.9M exploit, Onchain Perpetuals tops $1T volume. SK Crypto Regulation Delayed. Mining demand unaffected by Bitcoin price volatility - Abundant Mining CEO.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently at $87,000 with no change, while bitcoin dominance stands at 59.0% with no change. Ethereum is at $2,975 showing a 1% increase, Binance Coin at $855 with a 1% increase, and Solana at $124 with no change. Tom Lee recently purchased an additional $130 million in ETH for Christmas, maintaining $1 billion in cash reserves for the upcoming year. REKT drinks and WorldStarHipHop hint at a collaboration. BlackRock's BUIDL reaches $100 million in dividends and surpasses $2 billion in assets. Metaplanet acquires 4,279 bitcoin, totaling 35,102 BTC. Unleash Protocol faces a $3.9 million exploit with funds funneled through Tornado Cash. Onchain Perpetuals witness over $1 trillion in monthly volume as traders pursue leverage. South Korea's crypto regulations postponed due to stablecoin rule deadlock. Abundant Mining CEO suggests that Bitcoin price fluctuations have not hindered mining demand.
